audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Moses/Aaron roles, Pharaoh hardening, staff-serpent sign, Nile blood sign, magicians, and Pharaoh's refusal.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, See, I have made you as Divine to Pharaoh, and Aaron your brother shall be your prophet.
You shall speak all that I command you, and Aaron your brother shall speak to Pharaoh, that he send the children of Israel from his land.
But I myself will harden Pharaoh's heart, and I will multiply my signs and wonders in land of Egypt.
Pharaoh will not listen to you, and I will put my hand upon Egypt and bring out my hosts, my people children of Israel, from land of Egypt by great judgments.
Egypt shall know that I am YHWH when I stretch out my hand over Egypt and bring out the children of Israel from their midst.
Moses and Aaron did so; as YHWH commanded them, so they did.
Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron eighty-three years old, when they spoke to Pharaoh.
YHWH said to Moses and Aaron, saying,
When Pharaoh speaks to you, saying, Give for yourselves a wonder, then you shall say to Aaron, Take your staff and throw it before Pharaoh; it will become a serpent/dragon.
Moses and Aaron came to Pharaoh and did so, as YHWH commanded; Aaron threw his staff before Pharaoh and his servants, and it became a serpent/dragon.
Pharaoh also called the wise men and sorcerers, and they also, the magicians of Egypt, did so with their secret arts.
Each threw down his staff, and they became serpents/dragons; but Aaron's staff swallowed their staffs.
Pharaoh's heart was strong/hardened, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.
YHWH said to Moses, Pharaoh's heart is heavy; he refuses to send the people.
Go to Pharaoh in the morning; look, he goes out to the water. Stand to meet him on bank of Nile, and take in your hand the staff that turned into serpent.
Say to him, YHWH, Divine of Hebrews, sent me to you, saying, Send my people so they may serve/worship me in wilderness; and look, you have not listened until now.
Thus says YHWH: By this you shall know that I am YHWH. Look, I strike with staff that is in my hand upon waters in Nile, and they shall turn to blood/life.
The fish in Nile shall die, and Nile shall stink, and Egypt will be weary of drinking water from Nile.

YHWH said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Take your staff and stretch your hand over waters of Egypt, over their rivers, canals, pools, and every gathering of their waters, and they shall become blood/life; there shall be blood/life in all land of Egypt, even in wood and stone vessels.
Moses and Aaron did so, as YHWH commanded. He lifted staff and struck waters in Nile before eyes of Pharaoh and eyes of his servants, and all waters in Nile turned to blood/life.
The fish in Nile died, and Nile stank, and Egypt could not drink water from Nile; and blood/life was in all land of Egypt.
But magicians of Egypt did so with their secret arts, and Pharaoh's heart was strong/hardened, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken.
Pharaoh turned and went into his house, and did not set his heart even to this.
All Egypt dug around Nile for water to drink, for they could not drink from waters of Nile.
Seven days were fulfilled after YHWH struck the Nile.

translation_notes: Tannin may be serpent/dragon; preserve mythic and Egyptian-symbolic resonance without overclaiming.
Hardening terms begin to alternate: here heavy/strong language should be tracked carefully in second-pass lexical audit.
Nile judgment confronts the river as imperial life-source after Pharaoh used it for death in chapter 1.
safety_note: The sign targets Pharaoh's oppressive refusal and Egypt's imperial system, not Egyptians as an ethnicity. Ecological harm in the signs should be read as apocalyptic/legal confrontation with oppression, not as celebration of suffering.
